amendments.body: Four "resume from failed run" tests in `tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs` are broken by the retry limit increase. The tests `resume_from_failed_run_skips_completed_stages` (line 3027), `resume_from_failed_docs_change_run_skips_completed_stages` (line 1033), `resume_from_failed_ci_improvement_run_skips_completed_stages` (line 1101), and `resume_from_failed_quick_dev_run_skips_completed_stages` (line 1407) all use `with_transient_failure(stage, 3)` but `TransportFailure` max_attempts is now 5. The 4th attempt succeeds, so `assert!(first_result.is_err())` fails. Fix: bump the transient failure count from 3 to 5 in each of these tests so the run exhausts retries and fails as expected.
amendments.rationale: Verified by running the tests: each fails with `assertion failed: first_result.is_err()` at the expected assertion line. These are existing tests that were not updated to match the new retry limits.

amendments.body: The same four broken "resume from failed run" tests also lack `start_paused = true` on their `#[tokio::test]` attribute. With the new exponential backoff, each test incurs ~35 seconds of real-time delay (5s + 10s + 20s between retries). All four tests need `#[tokio::test(start_paused = true)]` to avoid real wall-clock backoff delays in CI.
amendments.rationale: Verified: `resume_from_failed_run_skips_completed_stages` took 35.03s and `resume_from_failed_docs_change_run_skips_completed_stages` took 35.02s. Even after fixing the transient failure count, these tests will each add ~60s+ of real time (5s+10s+20s+40s backoff for 5 attempts) without `start_paused`.

payload.summary: The implementation is solid across its four main changes: (1) retry limit increases are correctly applied in `retry_policy.rs`, (2) timeout enforcement via `tokio::time::timeout` in `process_backend.rs` correctly wraps I/O + process wait with proper SIGKILL cleanup, (3) the new `BinaryNotFound` failure class is properly integrated (enum variant, serde, as_str, terminal retry rule, classify_exit_failure for both spawn NotFound and exit 127), and (4) exponential backoff in the engine retry loop is correctly computed and capped. However, 4 existing "resume from failed run" tests are broken: they configure only 3 transient failures but TransportFailure now allows 5 attempts, so the run succeeds instead of failing. These same tests also lack `start_paused = true`, causing ~35s real-time backoff delays per test.
